name: OpenAI Whisper API
what_it_does: OpenAI's hosted Whisper API endpoint that transcribes or translates audio files to text using the Whisper large-v2 model, supporting 57 languages with high accuracy.
use_cases: Transcribing meeting recordings or voice memos into structured text for downstream processing
Building voice-to-action agents that convert spoken commands to structured instructions
Multilingual content pipelines that need transcription plus translation to English
Automated subtitling and captioning with timestamped word-level output
Customer support call analysis and summarization workflows
not_for: Real-time streaming transcription requiring sub-second latency (API processes complete files)
Audio files longer than 25MB without pre-splitting (hard limit per request)
On-premise deployments where data cannot leave your environment
best_when: An agent needs reliable, multi-language transcription with minimal setup and can tolerate file-upload latency rather than streaming.
avoid_when: You need live streaming transcription, speaker diarization, or have strict data residency requirements preventing upload to OpenAI.

auth.notes: Uses OpenAI API key passed as Authorization: Bearer header. Same key used across all OpenAI APIs. No per-endpoint scoping — full account access with any key.

pricing.free_tier_limits: $5 in free credits for new accounts
pricing.notes: Pricing is per audio minute, rounded to the nearest second. Costs are predictable for file-based workloads. Free credits expire after 3 months.

performance.rate_limits: 500 RPM and 25 requests per minute by default on tier 1; scales with account tier

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: OpenAI returns structured JSON errors with type, code, message, and param fields. Audio-specific errors (invalid file format, size exceeded) are clearly described with actionable messages.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency keys. Each request is independent; retrying produces a new transcription job. Results are deterministic for the same audio input.
known_agent_gotchas: 25MB hard file size limit — agents must split large audio files before submitting, which requires knowing the audio bitrate
No streaming — agents block until the full file is transcribed; long files can stall agent loops for 30+ seconds
Language must be specified or auto-detected; misdetection on short clips can silently return wrong-language transcripts
The response_format parameter defaults to json but verbose_json is needed for timestamps — agents often miss this
File must be uploaded as multipart/form-data, not JSON body — many generic HTTP clients need explicit configuration

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. API keys have no scope restriction — a leaked key grants full OpenAI account access. OpenAI recommends using project-scoped API keys (newer feature) to limit blast radius. Audio data is processed in OpenAI's US infrastructure with SOC2 compliance.
